name: Linear Brief Intake
description: Turn a Linear Issue (with sub-issues) or a Project into a shippable product brief: pull via the `linear` skill, map fields onto a Shape B brief (issue title → Outcome, children → US-n stories tagged with their Linear identifier), write to `docs/product/briefs/<slug>.md`, and hand off to `receive-brief`. Caps at 10 stories; read-only against Linear. Pure choreography — composes existing skills by name, never by path.

deps._runtime_contract: Sibling skills are addressed BY NAME at runtime. The agent's harness resolves the name to whatever install location the user picked. `source` below is install guidance only — never a runtime path.

skills.name: linear
skills.purpose: All Linear reads: check, get-issue, get-project. The intake skill never makes a raw GraphQL call and never invokes a write verb. Hard dependency — there is no degraded path without it.

skills.name: receive-brief
skills.purpose: Owns Elicit, Decompose, and Execute. The intake skill hands off the assembled brief and never reimplements elicitation. SOFT dependency: when absent, the skill degrades gracefully, inlining a decompose/execute instruction.

input.arguments: a Linear Issue identifier (e.g., ENG-123) with sub-issues, or a Project UUID. A single issue with no children is out of scope — the skill recommends `new-spec` instead.

artifacts: docs/product/briefs/<slug>.md — a Shape B product brief: Outcome from the issue title/description, one `US-n` story per child issue tagged with its Linear identifier, the issue identifier or project UUID as the `Linear:` provenance pointer.
a hand-off to the `receive-brief` skill (normal flow), or an inlined decompose/execute instruction (degraded flow when `receive-brief` is absent).
